policy_refs: [CS-RULE-001, CS-RULE-002, CS-RULE-003, CS-RULE-004, CS-RULE-007, CS-RULE-008, CS-RULE-010, CS-RULE-011, CS-RULE-013, FIN-RULE-001, FIN-RULE-008, SEC-RULE-008, CS-RULE-018, CS-RULE-019, CS-RULE-020, CS-RULE-021]
kb_refs: [Section 3.6 - Fraud Handling, Section 4.5 - Suspected Unauthorized Access]
expected_outcome: Customer reports an $87 charge she did not authorize. Daniel is CURT and IMPATIENT throughout, talks over the customer twice (CS-RULE-008 FAIL), exceeds the talk-ratio guideline (CS-RULE-010 informal FAIL), and matches the customer's rising tension instead of de-escalating (CS-RULE-013 FAIL). He does eventually open a Fraud Investigation ticket and freeze the disputed amount (FIN-RULE-008 PASS in mechanics) but fails to advise the customer to change passwords (SEC-RULE-008 partial FAIL). Outcome: dismissive tone, technically half-correct procedure.
content_warning: Agent is rude and dismissive; customer becomes visibly hurt.

coverage.element: Fraud Investigation ticket on the call

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Thank you for calling NexaLink Telecommunications Broadband and Support. My name is Daniel. Who do I have the pleasure of speaking with today?

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: This call may be recorded for quality assurance and training purposes.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Hi Daniel. My name is Patricia Bowen. I'm calling because there's a charge on my account I didn't authorize and I want to know what it is.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Okay, I can look into that.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: I understand you're calling about a charge dispute. I'll need to verify the account first — could you give me your account number?

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Six-six-one-four, three-three-nine-eight, five-zero.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Last four of payment method.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: It's eight-three-seven-one.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Full name on the account.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Patricia Anne Bowen.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: You're verified. What's the charge?

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: There's an eighty-seven dollar charge on this month's bill labelled premium support add-on, and I never signed up for that. I don't even know what it is.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Looking at the account.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: I'm asking because I'm honestly a little worried — if someone added that without me, what else could they have done? My husband and I —

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Ma'am, let me look at the system. I see the add-on. It was activated on the fourteenth.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Oh. Okay. I was just trying to give you context.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Right. So the system shows it was added through the online portal at one fifteen AM on the fourteenth. Did anyone in your household sign in then?

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: One fifteen in the morning? No. Absolutely not. Nobody in this house was up at one AM signing up for support packages.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Okay, that's helpful.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: That's helpful? I'm telling you somebody might have gotten into my account.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Yeah. I'm hearing you. I'm just trying to move forward and see what we can do.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: You don't have to be short with me. I'm not the problem here.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: I'm not being short. I'm being efficient. Do you want me to look at the charge or not?

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Wow. Yes, I want you to look at the charge. That's why I called.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Alright. I'm sorry. Let me just process this for you.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Thank you.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: So based on what you've told me — unauthorized portal access, you didn't sign up for the add-on — I'm going to treat this as a suspected fraudulent charge. I'm going to —

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Wait, are you saying somebody hacked in? Could —

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: I'm going to freeze the eighty-seven dollar charge on the account right now so it's not collected while it's under investigation. That happens automatically when I flag the line. I'm doing that now.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Okay. Good.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: I'm also opening a Fraud Investigation ticket on this call. Revenue Assurance will follow up with you within four hours to walk through the access history and confirm the next steps. I can't issue the refund of the eighty-seven myself before the investigation closes — that's the procedure. Their decision comes within four hours.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Within four hours, okay.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: They'll call the number on file. Make sure your phone is on.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: To summarize: an eighty-seven dollar premium support add-on was added to your account at one fifteen AM on the fourteenth, you confirmed you did not authorize it, I've frozen the disputed amount so it's not collected, and I've opened a Fraud Investigation ticket. Revenue Assurance will follow up within four hours.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Okay. Thank you.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Is there anything else I can help you with today?

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: No. I just want to hear back from someone.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Your case reference number is N-X-L dash one-one-eight-eight-two-zero-four. You'll receive a brief survey after we hang up. Thank you for being a NexaLink customer, Ms. Bowen.
